title: Vegan Mapo Tofu
summary: Mapo tofu is the heart and soul of Sichuanese cuisine and is the perfect vehicle for punchy and bold Sichuanese flavours. This vegan version is incredible.

ingredients.label: 2 Tbsp Fermented Black Beans, Rinsed In Cold Water And Patted Dry
ingredients.name: Fermented Black Beans

ingredients.label: 2 Tbsp Fermented Black Beans, Rinsed In Cold Water And Patted Dry
ingredients.name: Bean

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Sichuan Peppercorns, Toasted & Ground Into Powder
ingredients.name: Sichuan Peppercorn

ingredients.label: Cornflour Slurry (2 Tbsp Cornflour Mixed With 2 Tbsp Water)
ingredients.name: Cornflour

method.description: Gently cut the silken tofu into 2 ½ cm cubes. Get a large saucepan of salted water (the water should be salty like the ocean) over a medium heat. Simmer the tofu on low for 10 minutes then carefully strain.

While the tofu is simmering, get a wok over a high heat and drizzle in the vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the fermented black beans and sugar. Stir continuously until they become fragrant then lower the heat to medium

Add the doubanjiang, stirring continuously so that the doubanjiang doesn’t dry out and stick to the wok (add water to the mixture if it becomes too dry). Add the vegan mince and fry until slightly crisped.

Add 500ml (17fl oz) of water to the wok and then add your strained simmered silken tofu. Be very careful not to break the tofu cubes when incorporating into the other ingredients. At this point add your chilli oil, spring onion, garlic, ginger and 2 tsp of Sichuan peppercorn powder. Mix gently.
Add the cornstarch slurry and gently incorporate. Simmer for a few minutes over a low heat and finish by sprinkling more Sichuan peppercorn powder on top. Serve with rice.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
